units.text: Yes — that’s the issue.
The Recovery Key is for end-to-end encryption key backup / session recovery, not an access token for the Matrix Client‑Server API.
Using it as `access_token` will produce exactly what you’re seeing (`M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`).
To get a real access token (do this locally in Element; don’t paste it into chat):
In Element Desktop: `Settings` → `Help & About` → look for `Access Token` (copy).
If you don’t see it, check `Settings` → `Advanced` (some builds expose developer/debug info there), or enable a developer/debug view if your Element build supports it.
Then update `config/cce.json` with a local editor (so it doesn’t hit shell history), e.g.:
`nano config/cce.json`
replace only the `access_token` value (no `Bearer ` prefix, no spaces, no line breaks)
save
After that, tell me “token updated” and I’ll rerun the safe `whoami` check and then list joined rooms to help you pick the correct `room_id`.
`whoami` is still failing with `401 M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN` (“Token is not active”), so I can’t list joined rooms or discover `room_id` yet.
This means the value currently in `config/cce.json` is still not a valid Matrix Client‑Server access token for the `homeserver_url` you have configured.
What to check locally (no secrets in chat):
Confirm `homeserver_url` is correct for the account you logged into in Element (e.g.
`https://matrix-client.matrix.org` vs `https://matrix.org`, or a different homeserver entirely).
Confirm the access token you copied is actually an **Access Token** (Element’s “Access Token” field), not recovery key / secure backup / session key.
Make sure the token wasn’t copied with surrounding quotes or truncated (it should be a long opaque string; typically no spaces/newlines).
If you tell me the bot’s homeserver domain (just the domain, no token) and which Element screen you copied the token from, I can narrow down the exact correct place to get an active token.
Once `whoami` succeeds, I’ll immediately list candidates with only:
`room_id`
room name (if available)
bot joined: true

turns.answer:
Yes — that’s the issue. The Recovery Key is for end-to-end encryption key backup / session recovery, not an access token for the Matrix Client‑Server API. Using it as `access_token` will produce exactly what you’re seeing (`M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`).

To get a real access token (do this locally in Element; don’t paste it into chat):

- In Element Desktop: `Settings` → `Help & About` → look for `Access Token` (copy).
- If you don’t see it, check `Settings` → `Advanced` (some builds expose developer/debug info there), or enable a developer/debug view if your Element build supports it.

Then update `config/cce.json` with a local editor (so it doesn’t hit shell history), e.g.:
- `nano config/cce.json`
- replace only the `access_token` value (no `Bearer ` prefix, no spaces, no line breaks)
- save

After that, tell me “token updated” and I’ll rerun the safe `whoami` check and then list joined rooms to help you pick the correct `room_id`.

This means the value currently in `config/cce.json` is still not a valid Matrix Client‑Server access token for the `homeserver_url` you have configured.

What to check locally (no secrets in chat):

- Confirm the access token you copied is actually an **Access Token** (Element’s “Access Token” field), not recovery key / secure backup / session key.

If you tell me the bot’s homeserver domain (just the domain, no token) and which Element screen you copied the token from, I can narrow down the exact correct place to get an active token. Once `whoami` succeeds, I’ll immediately list candidates with only:
